title: readline: set_auto_history() writes the module-global should_auto_add_history flag without synchronization
description: readline.c gates auto-append-to-history on a file-scope C global `static int should_auto_add_history` (readline.c:825). readline.set_auto_history(bool) stores it with a plain unsynchronized write (readline.c:843) and is NOT @critical_section; it is read unsynchronized in call_readline() (readline.c:1584). Two threads calling set_auto_history() concurrently are a write/write data race on the global; a set_auto_history() writer vs an input()/readline() reader is the write/read variant. CPython-owned global (not libreadline, not the readlinestate struct); fix is FT_ATOMIC relaxed, mirroring the sibling _history_length global.
sites: Modules/readline.c:825 (def: static int should_auto_add_history = 1 -- file-scope C global, NOT in readlinestate)
Modules/readline.c readline_set_auto_history_impl:843 (WRITE, 4 bytes: should_auto_add_history = _should_auto_add_history; clinic function is NOT @critical_section)
Modules/readline.c call_readline:1584 (READ: if (should_auto_add_history && n > 0) -- unsynchronized read on the interactive input path)
race_kind: unsynchronized file-scope C global int flag (plain write in a non-@critical_section setter; plain read on the input path); write/write between two setters, write/read between setter and call_readline()
shared_state: should_auto_add_history (static int in Modules/readline.c, process-global, CPython-owned; controls whether typed lines are auto-added to readline history)
severity: low (value-benign 4-byte boolean flag; no refcount/pointer/allocation, no crash or memory-safety hazard; genuine TSan data race on CPython-owned shared state reachable from pure Python)
repro_reliability: deterministic (exit 66 on every run; 2/2 consecutive runs flagged the same write/write pair on global 'should_auto_add_history' at readline.c:843)
suggested_fix: make should_auto_add_history use FT_ATOMIC relaxed access matching the sibling _history_length global: FT_ATOMIC_STORE_INT_RELAXED at the :843 write and FT_ATOMIC_LOAD_INT_RELAXED at the :1584 read. @critical_section is the wrong tool (process-global, not per-module-object state; would not order the write against the lockless call_readline() read).
notes: CPython's OWN module global, not GNU libreadline internal state (no _rl_*/rl_*) and NOT part of the per-module readlinestate heap block -- fully fixable inside Modules/readline.c, distinct from the thread-unsafe-C-library class (gh-127081, locale/setlocale, libreadline reentrancy). Incomplete free-threading migration: the sibling flag _history_length (readline.c:320) was converted to FT_ATOMIC_{LOAD,STORE}_INT_RELAXED (readline.c:361/420/450/467) and the completer/hook pointer setters were made @critical_section, but should_auto_add_history was left as a bare global and set_auto_history was left non-@critical_section. Confirmed still plain on upstream main.
cross_check_vs_TSAN_0016: DISTINCT variable and DISTINCT fix from TSAN-0016 / gh-153291 (PR gh-153362, 'Fix data race in readline.get_completer() and get_pre_input_hook()'). gh-153291 = readlinestate POINTER fields (state->completer/pre_input_hook, PyObject* in per-module heap state) whose getters lacked @critical_section, with a latent borrowed-ref/UAF angle; its patch does NOT touch should_auto_add_history. This finding = a plain file-scope static int flag, no pointer/refcount/UAF, fix is FT_ATOMIC not @critical_section. SAME broad class (unsynchronized CPython-owned readline module state, incomplete FT migration, same file) but a SEPARATE, still-open gap not covered by gh-153291.

related_issues.note: readline get_completer/get_pre_input_hook FT race (PR #153362) -- DISTINCT field (should_auto_add_history static int, not readlinestate pointer); same incomplete readline FT migration. Fold into that cleanup or umbrella.

assessment: Real, low-severity, NEW field (module-global static int should_auto_add_history, plain write). Distinct from #153291 (pointer getters) but same readline FT-migration class. Fix: FT_ATOMIC. Fold into the readline cleanup rather than file standalone.
confirmed_unfixed_on: local checkout /home/danzin/projects/cpython @ 4f3be1b5777 (newer than the build) -- should_auto_add_history still a plain `static int` (readline.c:825), written unsynchronized at :843 in a non-@critical_section set_auto_history, read unsynchronized at :1591
